Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Patchy AA: spontaneous regrowth in 50-80% within 1 year; alopecia totalis/universalis (AT/AU): spontaneous full regrowth <10%; chronic AA (>10 years): spontaneous remission rare
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Baricitinib (Olumiant): 38.8% achieving ≥80% scalp coverage (SALT score ≤20) at 36 weeks vs 2% placebo (BRAVE-AA1); ritlecitinib (Litfulo): 23.4% SALT ≤10 at 24 weeks (ALLEGRO); dupilumab: negative Phase 3 for AA (not effective despite positive for AD)
remission.gap_size: Very large — JAK inhibitors are transformative but no approved treatment existed before June 2022; most patients previously managed with immunosuppressants with low efficacy and side effects
remission.barrier_detail: Baricitinib (2022) and ritlecitinib (2023) are the first approved treatments specifically for severe AA; prior to this, intralesional corticosteroids and systemic immunosuppressants (cyclosporine, methotrexate) provided variable and non-durable response; cost ($20-25K/year) and durability questions on treatment cessation are key barriers
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — ritlecitinib (Litfulo) FDA approved for AA (second JAK inhibitor); 2022 baricitinib (Olumiant) — first FDA-approved treatment for AA; prior systemic treatments used off-label
remission.outdated_treatments: Systemic corticosteroids as primary treatment for AT/AU (temporary regrowth with rapid relapse; significant systemic side effects without disease modification); PUVA phototherapy (modest efficacy, carcinogenic risk, largely replaced by JAK inhibitors); contact sensitisation (DPCP application) — effective but cumbersome
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Scalp massage (promotes blood flow, some case reports); selenium (early signal in autoimmune hair loss); zinc supplementation (deficiency associated with AA — repletion may help); melatonin topical (very small studies); low-level laser therapy (LLLT — modest evidence for stimulating regrowth)
remission.frontier_signal: Ritlecitinib (first JAK inhibitor approved specifically in AA — JAK3/TEC inhibitor, may have long-term durability advantage); deuruxolitinib (Phase 3 AA); brepocitinib (Phase 3); IL-4R blockade (dupilumab failed Phase 3 but IL-13-only targeting under study); stem cell-based follicular transplantation
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
